rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. MOATS ET AL VS. MICHAEL TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of And Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is CONTINUED to April 24, 2025 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NKECHI EMERUWA-NEUBERG VS. FRANK LUCCHETTI ET AL

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. FARMERS INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is DENIED. Plaintiff has not met the requirement of showing that her claims arise out of Defendant's "conduct as an individual of a trade, business, or profession" under CCP 483.010(c). Paragraph 1 of Plaintiff's SAC clearly states that: "This action arises from Defendant Andrea Chynowith's breaches of her residential lease agreement ." The lease agreement attached to the SAC is also clearly labeled as a "Residential Lease Agreement." Further, Plaintiff fails to cite any statutory authority for issuing a writ as to the settlement funds or funds in the IRA account. See 487.010(c). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HAPPY 42ND AVE PARTNERS, LLC, A SOUTH DAKOTA VS. RYAN DRUM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with and without leave to amend. As a preliminary matter, the court notes that this matter was continued from April 8, 2025 to April 14, 2025 for Plaintiff to provide a court courtesy copy of their opposition by April 8 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Plaintiff failed to do so. Consequently, the filed opposition was not considered. The demurrer to the First Cause of Action for Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts that tend to show the probability of future economic benefit, rather than a mere speculative expectancy. (See Youst v. Longo (1987) 43 Cal.3d 64.) For example, it is not clear whether Drum was the only bidder. The demurrer to the second cause of action for Negligent Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting a special relationship between Plaintiff and each individual defendant. The demurrer to the third cause of action for violations as to Civil Code sections 2924g and 2024h is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting a violation of each statute with reference to specific subdivisions. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend with respect to section 2924m. (See Civil Code Section 2924m(j).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HAPPY 42ND AVE PARTNERS, LLC, A SOUTH DAKOTA VS. RYAN DRUM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part with leave to amend. Defendant's motion to strike paragraph 24 and paragraph 2 of the prayer for relief is denied. Plaintiff alleges facts supporting a claim for punitive damages by alleging Defendants knowingly submitted a fraudulent bid under penalty of perjury in order to obtain the subject property. Defendant's motion to strike paragraph 7 of the prayer for relief requesting attorney's fees is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege entitlement to attorney fees either by contract, statute, or law. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. rulings.Case Title: MIREN R ALVAREZ VS. PROVIDENT FUNDING ASSOCIATES, L.P.. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with and without leave to amend. The demurrer to the 1st Cause of Action for violation of Civil Code section 2923.5 is sustained with leave to amend. The Notice of Default in this action was recorded pursuant to Civil Code section 2923.55, not section 2923.5. (See Def.'s RJN, Exh. 3.) The demurrer to the 2nd Cause of Action for Fraud is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's fraud claim appears to be based on the underlying premise that the lender, Defendant Provident Funding Associates LP, is the only party entitled to enforce the Note and foreclose on the property (Complaint, 30, 32), and that therefore, the Notice of Default was invalid. This premise is undermined by Civil Code section 2924 and the recorded Substitution of Trustee. Civil Code section 2924(a)(1) provides that a "trustee, mortgagee, or beneficiary, or any of their authorized agents" may initiate the foreclosure process. In this case, the lender substituted Defendant ZBS Law LLP as trustee and recorded the Substitution of Trustee on November 3, 2023. (Def.'s RJN, Exh. 2). Plaintiff fails to allege any facts supporting her conclusory statement that Defendants "falsely misrepresented that the Notice of Default was validly executed." (Complaint, 36.) With respect to the Declaration of Due Diligence, Plaintiff fails to describe with specificity the material representation and her reliance on that misrepresentation. As noted above, Plaintiff cites violation Civil Code section 2923.5. However, the declaration was filed pursuant to section 2923.55. The demurrer to the 3rd Cause of Action for Intentional Misrepresentation is sustained with leave to amend for the same reasons as the second cause of action. The demurrer to the 4th Cause of Action for violation of Civil Code Sec. 2923.6 is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action based on her "willingness to execute a modification" of her loan, which she claims was an option she was not given. Civil Code section 2923.6 does not grant a right to a loan modification. (See, Mabry v. Super. Ct. (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 208, 222 & fn. 9; see also, Hamilton v. Greenwich Investors XXVI, LLC (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1602, 1617.) Because Plaintiff has not indicated any facts in opposition that could support a claim for violation of section 2923.6, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The demurrer to the 5th Cause of Action for violation of Civil Code 1572 is sustained with leave to amend for the same reasons as the second cause of action. The demurrer to the 6th Cause of Action for violation of Business and Professions Code section 17200 is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff's cause of action is predicated on the conduct alleged in the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 5th causes of action. The demurrer to the 7th Cause of Action for violations of the Federal Truth In Lending Act is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must specifically allege what charges she alleges Defendants failed to include or disclose on the TIL statement. (See Complaint, 69.) The Court cannot determine whether this cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations, as Defendants contend, with the vague and conclusory allegations alleged. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: VALSTOCK VENTURES, LLC VS. TRUNG NGUYEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Trung Nguyen
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 14, 2025 line 7. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT TRUNG NGUYEN is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall serve complete verified responses without objection to the Special Interrogatories (Set One), Request for Production of Documents and Things (Set One), Form Interrogatories-General (Set One), and Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $960. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252].
